item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: Flickr
License: CC BY 2.0
Title: polaroid wall
Author: Shimelle Laine
Release year: 2008

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do not believe that image was taken in '76. looks 90's

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I like it

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer stopped. Edits made.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ show block
– hide block

I like that almost all Class of '76 articles are linked to each other. It gives it a nice wikiwalk feel. It is missing one link from an article that I think would fit, however.

I propose the following edit:

SCP-1423 affected subjects will attempt to revisit locations from their vacation, such as concerts, cities, amusement parks, farms, hometowns, and will attempt to contact other subjects whom they contacted during that period.

To this:

SCP-1423 affected subjects will attempt to revisit locations from their vacation, such as concerts, cities, amusement parks, farms, hometowns, and will attempt to contact other subjects whom they contacted during that period.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's a nostalgia-inducing polaroid that turns your nostalgia to hate? Yeah, not really feeling this one. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This strongly reminds me of the genre of emo (the real stuff, straight from (post-)hardcore).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this "class of 76" one better than 814. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 332 is the best riff on the theme, and 1833 makes this a redundancy with less adequate execution. The dream diary is the draw here, and it isn't drawing me. Downvoted this one; upvoted its stronger, healthier cousins.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the weakest Class of '76 SCP to me. I really like the idea, because well, I get grasping at nostalgia and trying to recapture those happy memories but finding it's never as good as you remember, but there's just something missing in the execution for me.

The font play in the diaries doesn't really work for me either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The logs are Syncope violating his mind

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed my vote after reading the tales and learning about the connection, the SCP is more interesting in that context.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Note that the phrase "We've had a great year, haven't we?" is present on both SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was neutral, it's well-written but the idea is just unappealing to me.

But the logs tipped me over to negative. They add nothing, we already knew victims increasingly lose their grip on reality, which would be forgiveable because of the striking imagery. But the font switch-up is both annoying to read and makes no sense in-universe.

Either we are supposed to believe that the Foundation transcriptionist painstakingly consider the most appropriate type-setting to match the scribblings of a madman; or that the original journal was kept on a computer and that the victim, seized by his fevered delusions and wrestling with recollections he knows to be preternaturally twisted, is taking the time to pause between words and artfully select the typeface that best illustrates his mad ramblings. It just doesn't fit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how this one is more bittersweet than scary, and that it's a great example of how a Thing That Makes You Crazy is not necessarily a bad thing, if written well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article set me off thinking about my own old regrets, which I suppose is sort of the point - the fear that you've never done enough, that you can't fix your mistakes, and that there's no future worth living if you can't fix your past.

I've got to say the changing font was a little goofy, but still +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Generally well-written, but the effect isn't all that great or original, it's kinda dull, and the logs read like "generic pissed off crazy person rambling." I'mma say no vote from me, just because it's not bad enough for me to dislike it. It's just kinda… There, yknow?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Perhaps 1976 is an anomalous waypoint, a spike in the infinite nexus of time where the focal point of all anomalies are tied. The undetectable, unseen temporal disturbances this year created merely by existing could have had far-reaching effects spanning across time and space and even other dimensions, affecting everything that is, was, and would be. This spike would ultimately cause a chain reaction that would result in the birth of all anomalous objects, locations and ideas, which would in turn cause several smaller, more noticeable chain reactions, resulting in the creation of several hundreds if not thousands of organizations and individuals of every species spanning the universe who in some way are connected to these anomalies, whether being one themselves or collecting them. Indeed, in some minute yet impossibly important way, the year 1976 may have been the catalyst for the Foundation itself.

Or it could just be one nifty cosmic coincidence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I regret now that I initially down voted. I reread it carefully and thought it was wonderful. Lovely. It reminds me of Stephen King in the best possible sense. I happen to like the changing fonts in the addendum. It suggest hand writing which I assume was the intent.
Hollow depraved eyes
Buy his soul back for a dime.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't forget SCP-332

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Between this and SCP-1833, I'm beginning to wonder if 1976 killed Roget's father or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had to downvote this, because it seems like the obvious half of some other, whole idea for an SCP item. As it stands now, there's nothing compelling; its a photo that makes you anomalous-ly nostalgic and then messes with your memory. If anything, the Thing What Makes You Obsessive aspect weakens it. I guess I feel like the primary effect has no hook, and I feel like most secondary effects I could think of would make it too generic SCP.

EDIT: I forgot to mention that the logs bothered me as well. I'm not sure if they were meant to imply anything, because they made absolutely no sense to me. Just seemed to be there to reinforce the Makes You Crazy aspect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was okay with this up until the logs, which just feel like goofiness for the sake of goofiness. Maybe I'm missing some intricate pattern or something, but it just becomes a mess. Feels more like The Road is trying to invade his diaries.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This ones kinda bittersweet. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually found this a bit eerie. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liking summer and being nostalgic isn't really a bias.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gave me a feel. As a person that often reminiscenced about good things from the past, this hit home for me.

And for the Sheldons that would say my vote was biased, I like how this SCP develops, as in, how it stops making the experiences pleasant to the user (hence the logs, right?)

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just kind of okay. The logs kept me from downvoting but don't seem suggestive or focused enough for an upvote either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems like I'm the only one who really liked this article. It goes from sad to disturbing and I like it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. I'm not interested. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only thing I feel really strong about when it comes to this article is how much the way the font changes mid sentence in the notes really bothers me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A lot of eh and a lot of meh. Nothing bad, but I felt nothing while reading it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not bad, but I feel like you could have done a lot more with the idea. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They are the dream diaries that the victim kept, I believe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unless I'm missing something super obvious, I don't get the notes entirely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have no strong feelings about this one way or the other.
